label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to the question about the garage sale. The question is asking what we know about garage sales based on the given text. First, I'll read the passage again to make sure I understand the details. The passage mentions that weekend outdoor garage sales have become very much a part of American culture. It says they are usually held in spring and fall when the weather is good. A homeowner might decide to clear out their home by having a garage sale and advertise in a local paper. The items on display are usually in good condition, and great buys can be found if one takes time to search. Examples of items include old magazines, books, etc. Also, if someone is moving or selling their house, they might practically give things away at a price far below their true worth. Now, looking at the options: (A) It is only held in the spring of the year. The passage says they are held "usually in the spring and fall," so it's not only in spring. So A is incorrect. (B) The goods on display are totally useless. The text says the goods are in good condition and can be great buys. So B is wrong. (C) Garage sales are popular with Americans. The passage states, "have now become very much a part of American culture," which implies popularity. So C seems correct. (D) All the goods are selling far below their real value.
